stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: OpenAI is launching 'Daybreak,' a new AI initiative focused on proactive cybersecurity.
Daybreak utilizes the Codex Security AI agent to build threat models from an organization's codebase.
The system automates the validation and detection of potential vulnerabilities before they can be exploited.

stories.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple has integrated end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging into the Messages app on iOS.
This feature allows iPhone users to have secure, encrypted chats with Android users.
The update, part of iOS 26.5, ensures that neither Apple nor Google can view the message content.

stories.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google successfully identified and neutralized a zero-day exploit.
The exploit was reportedly developed using Artificial Intelligence by cyber threat actors.
The vulnerability posed a significant risk, potentially allowing attackers to bypass two-factor authentication.

stories.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Data centers are consuming massive amounts of water (30 million gallons) without public awareness.
The article questions whether AI itself can provide a sustainable solution to the industry's high water usage.
It highlights a critical, often overlooked environmental impact of the rapidly expanding AI sector.

stories.title: Postmortem: TanStack NPM supply-chain compromise
stories.author: varunsharma07
bullet_points: The story details a supply-chain compromise affecting the TanStack NPM package.
This highlights critical vulnerabilities in the modern software development ecosystem.
The postmortem provides insights into how such compromises occur and how to mitigate them.

stories.title: Google says criminal hackers used AI to find a major software flaw
stories.author: donohoe
bullet_points: Criminal hackers reportedly leveraged Artificial Intelligence (AI) to discover a significant software vulnerability.
The news originates from Hacker News, indicating high interest within the tech community.
This story highlights the evolving threat landscape where AI is being weaponized in cyberattacks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its focus on conversational or creative AI applications.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance enhancement.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B parameter sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) operating over discrete tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to enable effective continuous DLMs with minimal adaptation to discrete domains.
This work bridges the gap between successful continuous generation methods (like those in image/video) and the discrete nature of language.

stories.title: Shepherd: A Runtime Substrate Empowering Meta-Agents with a Formalized Execution Trace
stories.author: Weiyan Shi
bullet_points: Introduces 'Shepherd,' a functional programming model designed to formalize meta-agent operations.
It maintains a Git-like execution trace of agent-environment interactions, allowing for state forking and replay.
Demonstrates significant performance gains, achieving 5x faster process forking than Docker and high prompt-cache reuse.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap between continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) and the discrete nature of language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective with minimal adaptation to discrete language domains.
This work represents a significant step toward applying powerful continuous generative modeling techniques to language tasks.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the deployment bottleneck of large MoE models by reducing storage and memory overhead.
Achieves performance comparable to dense Transformers while being optimized for resource-constrained end-side devices.

stories.title: Beyond Red-Teaming: Formal Guarantees of LLM Guardrail Classifiers
stories.author: Luca Arnaboldi
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM safety: current guardrail classifiers lack formal guarantees against harmful behavior.
The research proposes a novel verification approach by shifting analysis from the discrete input space to the classifier's pre-activation layer.
This work tackles the difficulty of formally defining 'harmful behavior' in a way that standard mathematical tools can handle.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the major bottleneck of MoE models: large parameter footprint hindering end-side deployment.
Achieves performance comparable to dense Transformers while maintaining low computational cost and small storage.

stories.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
stories.author: Yuhang Zang
bullet_points: Introduces WildClawBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate real-world, long-horizon agent performance.
Addresses a critical gap in current agent evaluation by moving beyond synthetic sandboxes and short-horizon tasks.
Features 60 human-authored, bilingual, and multimodal tasks that test agents in native runtime environments.

stories.title: RUBEN: Rule-Based Explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLM Systems
stories.author: Jarek Szlichta
bullet_points: Introduces RUBEN, a tool designed to generate minimal, rule-based explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLMs (RAGs).
Employs novel pruning strategies to efficiently distill complex LLM behavior into a concise set of governing rules.
Demonstrates practical applications of these rules for LLM safety, including testing training resiliency and adversarial robustness.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently available for macOS users in an experimental capacity.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model serving and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Yarbo is removing a previously mentioned intentional remote backdoor from its robot lawn mower.
The backdoor access, which could allow unauthorized reprogramming, is being eliminated.
Customers will now have the option to choose whether the remote access feature is installed.

stories.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Mira Murati's AI company, Thinking Machines, is developing 'interaction models'.
The goal of these models is to enable natural, collaborative interaction between humans and AI.
The technology will continuously process multimodal inputs, including audio and video.

stories.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Linux has recently experienced a second severe vulnerability within a short timeframe.
Urgent action is required as production-version patches are being released.
The news highlights ongoing security challenges within the Linux ecosystem.

stories.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Starlink has disabled its proprietary GPS-like location feature.
The shutdown is unlikely to halt research into alternative positioning systems.
The story highlights ongoing academic interest in developing GPS alternatives.

stories.title: Engineering Robustness into Personal Agents with the AI Workflow Store
stories.author: Wen Zhang
bullet_points: Challenges the dominant 'on-the-fly' planning paradigm for AI agents.
Argues that real-time synthesis bypasses crucial software engineering (SE) practices like rigorous testing and staged deployment.
Proposes a need to integrate disciplined SE processes to improve the robustness and reliability of personal AI agents.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the manual and ad hoc nature of data engineering, which is becoming a bottleneck in modern ML.
It proposes 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' using an autonomous agent.
The goal is to automate data sourcing, adaptation, validation, and iterative improvement for ML pipelines.

stories.title: Shields to Guarantee Probabilistic Safety in MDPs
stories.author: Sebastian Junges
bullet_points: The paper addresses the complexity of ensuring probabilistic safety in Markov Decision Processes (MDPs) using shielding techniques.
It introduces a formal framework that conservatively extends classical, deterministic shields to handle probabilistic safety guarantees.
Shielding is highlighted as a key model-based technique for guaranteeing the safety of autonomous agents.

stories.title: LoKA: Low-precision Kernel Applications for Recommendation Models At Scale
stories.author: Chunqiang Tang
bullet_points: The paper introduces LoKA, a method to apply low-precision arithmetic (like FP8) to large recommendation models (LRMs).
LRMs are numerically sensitive due to small matrix multiplications and normalization, making direct FP8 application problematic.
LoKA addresses the limitations of low-precision training in LRMs, which have been less adopted than in LLMs.

stories.title: AssayBench: An Assay-Level Virtual Cell Benchmark for LLMs and Agents
stories.author: Gabriele Scalia
bullet_points: Introduces AssayBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI agents in biological contexts.
The research focuses on building a 'virtual cell'—a computational model to simulate cellular behavior and accelerate biological discovery.
A key application is performing in silico phenotypic screens, allowing models to predict the effects of cellular changes in new scenarios.

stories.title: Variational Inference for Lévy Process-Driven SDEs via Neural Tilting
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of performing Bayesian inference on Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) driven by Lévy processes.
Lévy processes are highlighted as a suitable mathematical tool for modeling heavy-tailed phenomena and extreme events in fields like finance and climate science.
The proposed solution utilizes Neural Variational Inference (NVI) to overcome the scalability limitations of traditional Monte Carlo methods for this intractable problem.

stories.title: Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing external skills used by LLM agents for complex task solving.
Challenges the assumption that external skills must either be persistent guidance or internalized into the policy.
Proposes a new framework to handle skill usage given limited parametric capacity and varying skill contributions.

stories.title: Optimal and Scalable MAPF via Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport and Schrödinger Bridges
stories.author: Joseph W. Durham
bullet_points: The research addresses Anonymous Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) on graphs.
It reformulates MAPF as a Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport (MMOT) problem with a Markovian structure.
The key finding is that this MMOT problem can be reduced to a polynomial-sized Linear Program (LP) under specific conditions.

stories.title: Equivariant Reinforcement Learning for Clifford Quantum Circuit Synthesis
stories.author: Rob Cornish
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of synthesizing Clifford quantum circuits for quantum devices.
It frames the circuit synthesis as a Reinforcement Learning (RL) problem where an agent learns gate sequences.
A novel, equivariant neural network architecture is introduced to aid in the learning process.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the manual and ad hoc nature of data engineering in modern ML workflows.
It proposes 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' using an autonomous agent.
The goal is to automate the process of finding, adapting, and validating datasets for ML models.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of applying continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) to discrete language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective with minimal adaptation.
This work aims to bridge the gap between successful continuous generative models (used in images/video) and the discrete nature of language modeling.

stories.title: RubricEM: Meta-RL with Rubric-guided Policy Decomposition beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Tomas Pfister
bullet_points: Addresses the limitations of standard reinforcement learning (RL) when training complex, generative AI agents (e.g., research agents).
Proposes using 'rubrics' not just for final evaluation, but as an integral part of the learning process to guide policy decomposition.
Focuses on enabling reusable experience from complex, tool-augmented decision-making trajectories where ground-truth answers are unavailable.

stories.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
stories.author: Mohammad M. Ghassemi
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'visual ungroundedness' in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), where predictions rely solely on language priors.
Introduces BICR (Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking), a novel, model-agnostic method for estimating model confidence.
The method specifically aims to distinguish predictions shaped by visual input versus those driven purely by textual knowledge.

stories.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models
stories.author: Mohamed S. Abdelfattah
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM inference efficiency research: static computation budgets.
Proposes a novel approach: dynamic budget allocation for autoregressive decoding.
Aims to optimize computation by learning how much processing power to dedicate to each token based on its difficulty.

stories.title: DGPO: Beyond Pairwise Preferences with Directional Consistent Groupwise Optimization
stories.author: Wei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces DGPO, a new framework to improve Large Language Model (LLM) alignment.
Addresses limitations in current preference optimization by focusing on directional consistency and reasoning diversity.
DGPO aggregates supervision signals at the group level and uses multi-candidate comparisons for direction-aware alignment.

stories.title: UCLA discovers first stroke rehabilitation drug to repair brain damage (2025)
stories.author: bookofjoe
bullet_points: UCLA researchers have identified a potential drug for stroke rehabilitation.
The drug aims to repair brain damage caused by strokes.
The discovery is reported as a significant medical advancement.

stories.title: Popular Go library fsnotify raises supply chain alarms after maintainer access changes
stories.author: socket.dev via knedl
bullet_points: The popular Go library fsnotify is facing supply chain security concerns.
The alarms stem from recent changes in the maintainer's access to the project.
This highlights growing industry awareness regarding software supply chain vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Run Claude Code Locally for Free with Docker Model Runner
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article discusses running Claude Code locally.
It utilizes Docker Model Runner for local execution.
The primary appeal is providing a free alternative to cloud usage limitations.

stories.title: Gemini CLI: Google's Free AI Agent for Your Terminal 🚀
stories.author: dvalin99
bullet_points: Google has released a Gemini CLI tool.
This tool functions as a free AI agent accessible via the terminal.
It represents an integration of advanced AI into developer workflows.

stories.title: I Was About to Rewrite My Chat Router. The Bug Was Two Lines in a Prompt.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: A seemingly complex architectural bug in a chat router was traced back to a simple issue within the LLM prompt.
The core problem was the prompt instructing the model to disregard correct data returned by the search router.
The fix was minimal, involving only a change to a variable label, highlighting the sensitivity of LLM prompting.
